headline: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
summary: Author Dave Eggers criticized ChatGPT during a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. He believes the AI technology is 'silencing an entire generation.' Eggers, a prominent writer and arts advocate, conveyed his concerns directly to the company.

headline: Google Pixel 11a might get flagship Tensor G6 processor
summary: Leaks suggest the upcoming Pixel 11a will feature a flagship-grade Tensor G6 processor. This would be an improvement over the Pixel 10a, which disappointed users with the older Tensor G4. The Pixel 'a' lineup traditionally includes modern processors, making this a return to form.

headline: Google open-sources its 3D emoji for creators
summary: Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji, allowing users to incorporate them into their own creations. The company shared details about the design process behind these unique pictograms. This initiative was announced as part of World Emoji Day.

headline: GoPro Max 2 bundle discounted with $100 worth of accessories
summary: The GoPro Max 2 accessory bundle is currently discounted to $369 on Amazon. This deal includes $100 worth of accessories with the 360-degree camera. It offers a significant saving on a high-end action camera.

headline: Matt Damon wants new Jason Bourne film to emulate original trilogy
summary: Matt Damon provided an update on the new Jason Bourne movie, expressing a desire for it to 'Be like the first 3'. Work on the film continues with director Edward Berger, known for 'Conclave' and 'All Quiet on the Western Front'. Damon is reprising his iconic role.

headline: Today's Best Deals: PNY RTX 5070 OC, Apple AirPods 4, and more
summary: IGN highlights the top deals available for July 18, 2026. Featured discounts include the PNY RTX 5070 OC graphics card. Shoppers can also find deals on Apple AirPods 4 and The Dark Knight Trilogy.

headline: Valve engineers warn memory crisis is worsening for Steam Machines
summary: Steam Machine engineers at Valve have warned that the ongoing memory crisis in tech is 'still getting worse'. This issue is causing buyers to face already exorbitant prices for components. The grim outlook affects the future of hardware development.

headline: Call of Duty movie confirmed for Modern Warfare universe setting
summary: Paramount Pictures and Activision have confirmed the upcoming Call of Duty movie will be set in the Modern Warfare universe. The director officially made this statement. This decision aligns the film with the popular game series' storyline.

headline: England beats France 6-4 in thrilling World Cup third-place play-off
summary: England defeated France 6-4 in a thrilling third-place play-off match. Bukayo Saka scored a hat-trick during the game. This victory secures England's best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

headline: Andrew Tate and brother arrested in US on UK charges
summary: Andrew Tate and his brother have been arrested in the US following new charges in the UK. The Crown Prosecution Service has requested their extradition from the US. Their lawyer maintains that both brothers are innocent.

headline: Burnham to scrap digital ID, prioritize cost of living
summary: Incoming Prime Minister Burnham plans to scrap the digital ID policy. His government will instead focus on addressing the cost of living crisis. A spokesperson confirmed the administration will prioritize issues 'where people need it right now'.

headline: US and Iran exchange strikes following US deaths in Jordan
summary: US forces have targeted Iranian sites for the eighth consecutive night. This intensified action follows the deaths of two US service members in a Jordan attack. Iran claims it also fired drones at US military in Kuwait.
